Placeable
Slot: PRIMARY SECONDARY
Skill: 1H Blunt Atk Delay: 28
DMG: 14 Dmg Bonus: 35 AC: 5
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.
SV COLD: +10 SV POISON: +10
WT: 3.0 Size: SMALL
Class: ALL except BER
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 4 (Weapon: General)
Slot 2, Type 20 (Ornamentation)

i am interested on some info pls? it has to do with dual wield. i have a Goranga spiked club in primary since it can only go there dly 23 and Cane of Harmony in secondary dly 25 or 28 cant remember. are these 2 weps dly too far apart to get proper dual wield hits or am i okay with this set-up? for i have not spent much time looking into this as i guess i should have. thnk u

kd and this guy
QuoteReply
# Jun 20 2002 at 9:54 AM Rating: Decent
-REDACTED-
126 posts
Have one of these on my lvl 34 mnk. dualing it with a knuckle duster. Not sure about the give and take of speed vs main/off hand... but I really do think that I hit more often with same speed weapons in both hands. had 2 cane of tranquil, got kd, and noticed a bit of difference in hits made/connecting (aside from the speed of the kd being slower), got this with kd and hits are much more consistant.
Is this in my mind or some other reason... no real clue... just like to see 4 hits at once consistantly.

price i got it for was 2500 in ec on brell.

▲ Page top
RE: kd and this guy
QuoteReply
# Dec 09 2003 at 7:56 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1592344474330
Sage
3,705 posts
Its your imagination, primary and secondary are not tied together.
▲ Page top
yup yup
QuoteReply
# May 15 2002 at 4:20 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592757709211
22 posts
48 human monk here. Have this in offhand. I ALWAYS keep faster weapon in primary. This is great offhand. Great monk weapon although just a little bit heavy.

Primary = Master Wu's Trance Stick
Secondary = Etched Steel Baton
▲ Page top
RE: yup yup
QuoteReply
# Aug 05 2002 at 3:17 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592250854409
9 posts
Actually you shall put faster weapon in your off hand. The reason is if you put the slower weapon in your offhand, you may miss a duel wield chance because the delay for the slower weapon haven't meet. I always put the faster weapon in my monk's offhand, yes the primary might hit a little slower, but at least I'm not missing any chance to use my offhand.

Edited, Mon Aug 5 16:17:03 2002
▲ Page top
RE: yup yup
QuoteReply
# Aug 25 2002 at 8:06 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592759452672
75 posts
dude wtf are you on? offhand is supposed to be slower weapon, you want a primary with fast delay regardless of dmg and an offhand thats higher delay/dmg, or if you have like kd/ vs fighting baton ratio then i'd put KD in main hand because it's a lot better
▲ Page top
RE: yup yup
QuoteReply
# Sep 08 2002 at 10:58 AM Rating: Decent
-REDACTED-
126 posts
I guess it would make sense to put a fast weapon in off hand if you are still increasing skill in dual wield and have more than enough firepower with the main hand as well.

Or if you do not wish to take all the aggro that speed creates in main hand, slower and higher damage weapons are not bad.

But, yes, it is understood that a faster weapon in main hand takes more advantage of damage bonus. If the damage/delay on the faster one is comparable to the damage/delay on the offhand.

Practice is a good thing to do. When you get to DL. get you some fast delay/low damage weapons and practice your skills on others mobs if you cannot find group. Nearly all will allow you to DoT their kills. Is a great way to get 2hb and h2h up.
▲ Page top
WTS

View User Forum

hi i'm a level 14 druid and i think this weapon rocks it would definitively make a great weapon for me so i can melee monsters to death well yes i think it would be great do you think this weapon will be sold very often and if so how much maybe i can buy myself one oh well bye
▲ Page top
RE: wow
QuoteReply
# Dec 17 2001 at 12:22 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592344140492
366 posts
Actually I'd have to say that this wouldn't make a good weapon for you. If you like playing a druid and want to melee mobs to death you should be playing a ranger :P

At your level you should be looking for +wis (and later +svs) from your weapon. With the AC and ratio on this it really belongs in the hand of someone that is (and should be) getting hit.
▲ Page top
RE: wow
QuoteReply
# Jan 12 2002 at 11:01 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1591759018338
163 posts
I would have to disagree with this. At level 14 a druid should have a varity of ac, mana, and nice weapons. That is if you are going the twink route. A good set of items will take you to 26, after that start working on the wis
▲ Page top
RE: wow
QuoteReply
# Nov 14 2001 at 8:54 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592248526746
29 posts
Prices and rarity of items depend on one thing and one thing only: How old your server is.

The older your server is, the easier it will be to find items at low prices.
▲ Page top
RE: wow
QuoteReply
# Oct 01 2004 at 1:32 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1594573640660
175 posts
Another factor is Hot spots for farming. Each server most likely has zones that are hunted often,while other servers you can hear crickets outside. The Timorous Deep is rarely farmed on Bristlebane server(maybe one or two players in zone),while on other servers I play it's pretty active(5 to 20 players). This can affect the rarity of items that can only be found in those zones.
On Druzzil Ro,Bricks of Ethereal Energy are 20pp+ while on Bristlebane,they can be bought every day for 5 to 10pp each. I'm not sure if this is because of zone-farming or just from popularity of certain recipes.
▲ Page top

QuoteReply
# Oct 09 2001 at 10:21 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1593361349566
43 posts
Who drops this? Would love this in primary and jade mace in offhand. Is it possible to camp the spawn or is it too deep for a solid group of low-mid 50s?
▲ Page top
RE: drop
QuoteReply
# Oct 09 2001 at 10:56 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591750565886
36 posts
hmm not a good primary toy would perform much better offhand with the jade mace primary
▲ Page top
RE: drop
QuoteReply
# Oct 10 2001 at 3:03 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591763100862
12 posts
Problem is, this has a delay of 28 and a Jade Mace has a delay of 18, so you'll have no chance of dual wield on every other mace swing as the Baton will not yet be recycled. To be truly effective your off hand weapon should be equal to or faster than your Primary.
▲ Page top
RE: drop
QuoteReply
# Oct 10 2001 at 4:50 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
duel wield is NOT effected by speed of either weapon. they are independant of each other.
▲ Page top
RE: drop
QuoteReply
# Jan 16 2002 at 11:39 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591771028480
View User Forum
29 posts
Ummmmm, not until your skill of Dual Wield is over 200. Before that, secondary is dependant on primary. LOTS of posts and tests on that. Its a proven fact. Might be 250 DW, or 200 can't remember, but it is not until a high skill that it is independant!

BTW this would be a great ranger weapon, lots of damage, but delay wont keep all the aggro on you, but enough if you need to taunt off a caster.

just my 2cp worth
▲ Page top
RE: drop
QuoteReply
# Feb 08 2002 at 4:19 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591763939769
